mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in industrial production signals improving real activity and earnings breadth, causing higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the INDPRO trend is rising.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value 110.269 as of two months before 2006-03-15
failure_condition: Reject if rising 3-month INDPRO momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above a restrictive threshold raises equity discount rates and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5% or rises by more than 0.75pp over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value 4.49 as of one month before 2006-03-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered restrictive-rate observations deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or average triggered returns exceed the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation momentum and the federal funds rate rise together, markets price tighter policy and lower future margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth rises while FEDFUNDS rises over the same 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value 199.0 and FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value 4.49
failure_condition: Reject if joint CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS increases are not followed by below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate stronger household demand and near-term revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when RSAFS momentum is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value 362294.0 as of one month before 2006-03-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS momentum has a nonpositive coefficient in walk-forward return regressions or fails to beat unconditional 20-day returns by at least 10 bps on average.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in housing starts signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and future construction spillovers, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 6-month moving average after being above it in the prior month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot value 2276.0 as of two months before 2006-03-15
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside-crossing events are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if downside crossings have no negative average return effect.

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment combined with a high policy rate signals labor-market tightness under restrictive monetary policy, increasing wage and discount-rate pressure and causing negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is below 5.0% and FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value 4.8 and FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value 4.49
regime_hint: tight labor market with restrictive policy
failure_condition: Reject if observations with UNRATE below 5.0% and FEDFUNDS above 4.0% have positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rising while housing starts fall identifies a late-cycle divergence where manufacturing strength masks demand weakness, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is positive while HOUST 3-month change is negative
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO snapshot 110.269 and HOUST snapshot 2276.0 were visible at the decision time
failure_condition: Reject if the INDPRO-up and HOUST-down interaction does not produce lower 60-day returns than periods when both series rise, using a 5% significance threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll employment signals stronger income growth and earnings demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days when PAYEMS momentum improves.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 1-month change exceeds its trailing 12-month average change
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value 134789.0 as of one month before 2006-03-15
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS upside momentum events are followed by below-median 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward observations.
